query: I need a proper design doc before we start building the new payments service. rails 7, stripe connect, b2b marketplace model. draft the plan — scope, out-of-scope, key decisions, unknowns. don't write code yet, i need to align the team first
walk me through how you'd approach migrating our user auth from devise sessions to stateless jwt. 800k active users, live app. I want a written plan I can show my team before any code gets touched
thinking about adding graphql alongside our existing rest api for the mobile clients. before I commit I need the trade-offs and a recommended approach laid out as a plan — no code, just the thinking

our oncall has been paged 3x/week for the same stripe webhook double-processing issue. im done patching. lets step back and redesign this properly — i want a written proposal with scope, key decisions, and risks

help me think through whether to adopt temporal.io or build our own saga pattern on top of postgres. give me a concrete recommendation with reasoning and what evidence would change your mind — not a both-sides writeup

before i green-light the refactor of our permissions system (currently spread across 6 django apps), i need a written plan with what's in scope, what's explicitly out, and the 3-5 key decisions i'm signing off on
想把 billing module 從 monolith 拆出來做 microservice, 不要直接開始寫. 先給我完整的設計思路, 範圍, 風險, 跟關鍵決策. 我要批准後才開始實作

we're evaluating snowflake vs bigquery vs databricks for our new analytics warehouse. 5TB/month ingest growing 2x/year, need a clear recommendation with falsifiable criteria. no both-sides waffle, pick one and tell me why

i want to add multi-tenancy to our b2b saas. currently single-tenant postgres, 5 engineers, 3-month budget. give me the approach, scope, key decisions — then i'll decide whether to greenlight. no code yet

plan for introducing event sourcing to our order management module. i need scope, non-goals, risks, and the key architectural decisions written down before any coding. im not ready to implement, just to decide
theres a typo in README.md line 34, says 'baranus' instead of 'baransu'. fix it
this test in tests/test_auth.py line 127 is failing because the mock returns None but the assertion expects {}, just add a null check and move on
can you review the PR i just opened, #482, its 3 files around the retry logic. just checking for bugs, dont rewrite it
can you explain how our auth middleware was designed? i inherited this codebase from the previous team and want to understand why they chose jwt over sessions. not planning any changes, just learning
rename the variable userEmail to userEmailAddress across the whole codebase, its inconsistent between files right now. mechanical change, no logic shifts
migrate utils/date_helpers.js to typescript. just converting, no behavior change, just adding type annotations. no design decisions needed
refactor user_service.py variable naming — all the names are camelCase but the rest of the repo is snake_case. purely cosmetic, no logic changes
set up my local dev env per the README, something broke when i pulled latest main and now pytest wont even collect. just need it working again
add a unit test for the parseDate function in utils/date.ts, should cover the ISO string case on line 23 and the empty string case. the function itself is fine, just needs coverage
run the test suite and tell me whats red, then fix any obvious assertion-mismatch bugs. no big changes
